idea.hook: Which body in our solar system holds more liquid water than Earth?
facts: Jupiter's moon Europa is an ice-covered world, roughly the size of Earth's moon.
Beneath its 15-25 km thick icy crust, scientists believe a vast saltwater ocean exists, estimated to be up to 150 km deep.
This hidden ocean is estimated to contain more than *twice* the volume of water found in all of Earth's oceans combined (~2.7 billion cubic km vs. 1.35 billion cubic km).
idea.payoff: More water than Earth, hidden from sight, making Europa one of the best places to search for extraterrestrial life!
idea.title: Europa: Hidden Ocean, More Water Than Earth! 🌊🔬

scenes.text: Which body has MORE liquid water than Earth?
scenes.visual: A dark cosmic background with twinkling stars. A faint, icy moon silhouette begins to emerge.

scenes.text: Europa: Jupiter's ice-covered moon. Roughly the size of Earth's Moon.
scenes.visual: Close-up of Europa's icy, fractured surface. Earth's Moon appears briefly next to it for scale, then fades.

scenes.text: Beneath its 15-25 km thick icy crust, a vast saltwater ocean exists, up to 150 km deep.
scenes.visual: Cross-section animation of Europa. The ice crust is peeled back to reveal a deep blue ocean layer beneath. Depth markers appear.

scenes.text: This hidden ocean holds >TWICE the water of ALL Earth's oceans combined! (~2.7 vs 1.35 billion km³)
scenes.visual: A stylized comparison: a large, glowing blue sphere representing Europa's ocean appears next to a smaller blue sphere representing Earth's oceans.

scenes.text: Europa: More water than Earth, hidden from sight... A PRIME place to search for extraterrestrial life!
scenes.visual: An artistic rendition of Europa's subsurface ocean. Faint light from above, shadowy hydrothermal vents at the bottom, and a suggestion of microbial life (stylized glowing particles). Europa's full sphere then reappears, glowing faintly.

script.thumbnail_text: Europa: Home to MORE water than Earth?!
